messages.graphFeedback: Select a place and render graph. Click on graph for details.
messages.sectorHelp: Select a place and use the day-slider. For details click the solar sector.
messages.addPlaceHelp: Enter a place (may include an address), then 'Find Place' and 'Save Place' or click on the map to set latitude/longitude.
messages.noYearlyData: No yearly data available for selected place/year.
messages.insufficientData: Insufficient data for selected graph mode.

messages2.fillAndSave: Fill in values and click Save Place.
messages2.tipClickMap: Tip: click on the map to set exact latitude/longitude.

help.title: Help
help.intro: SolarOne Web calculates and visualizes solar data for places using browser-only storage.
help.quickStart1: Use World Map to view places and select/deselect them.
help.quickStart2: Use Add Place to geocode a location, adjust coordinates, and save.
help.quickStart3: Use Graphs to render Rise/Set, Max Elevation, and Length of Day.
help.quickStart4: Use Solar Sector to inspect sunrise-to-sunset azimuth sector by day.
help.data1: All data is stored in your browser (localStorage), no backend required.
help.data2: Starter places are seeded on first run; use Restore Starter Places if needed.
help.data3: Yearly solar values are precomputed and cached per place/year for performance.
help.noKey1: Maps use OpenStreetMap tiles (no billing key).
help.noKey2: Geocoding/timezone lookup uses public services and may be rate limited.
help.noKey3: Satellite map style is intentionally not included in no-key mode.
help.access1: Status and feedback messages are announced via live regions.
help.access2: Forms use labels and native controls for keyboard and screen-reader support.
help.access3: Use browser zoom and responsive layout for readability on different devices.

addPlace.geocodeErrorFallback: {error} You can still set coordinates manually or by map click.
addPlace.timezoneSet: Timezone set to {timezoneId}. DST {dst} by rule inference.
addPlace.timezoneErrorFallback: {error} Keep manual timezone/DST values or retry.
addPlace.savedAdded: Saved {name}. Added to world map with precomputed {year} solar data.
